resultRows.geneticAnalysis: A derived MCM6/LCT rs4988235 CT call is available for lactase-persistence context. This is not a diagnosis, symptom explanation, food-sensitivity result, diet recommendation, or supplement recommendation.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: This marker can provide lactose-related context, but symptoms, diet history, labs, environment, and professional review matter for nutrition decisions.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: No calibrated diet, weight-loss response, obesity-risk, meal-plan, supplement, metabolic, diet-history, activity-history, lab, or ancestry applicability model is supplied with this fixture.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: The report can describe supplied marker context and sample-report structure, but it should not make diet, supplement, weight-loss, metabolic, lab, or diagnosis conclusions without validated evidence.

sampleRows.item: Genetic profile and high-level action point page
sampleRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample action-points page labels the genetic profile as high-protein diet, shows a recommended macronutrient-ratio chart with Protein 35%, Carbs 45%, and Fat 20%, and shows high-level trans-fat, metabolic-response, food-addiction, fat-sensing, and resting-energy-expenditure action-point structure.
sampleRows.description: Visible sample text includes a recommended macronutrient-ratio graphic, high-protein diet, fat and carbohydrate ratio, trans-fat limit, gradual calorie reduction, food-addiction steps, fat-taste sensitivity, and regular exercise sections.
sampleRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Extracted from page 4 of the public GeneInformed DNA Diet sample PDF; this is observed sample output structure, not local diet, exercise, supplement, weight-loss, metabolic, lab, or treatment advice.

sampleRows.description: The public sample summary table shows this row on page 5, and the detailed trait page labels the section High protein intake; 17% of people have the displayed tendency.

sampleRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample appendix contains satiety-promoting behavior structure, fullness-promoting food categories, eating behavior prompts, snacking reduction, social eating, portion-size, and activity sections.
sampleRows.description: Visible appendix pages 17-20 provide generic behavior lists rather than genotype-specific variant rows.
sampleRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Extracted from Appendix A pages 17-20 of the public sample PDF as formal appendix structure only; do not convert to a local behavioral plan.

sampleRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample appendix includes emotional-eating education, awareness of when and why eating happens, regular meals, stress reduction, and exercise context.
sampleRows.description: Visible appendix page 21 presents educational emotional-eating text and bullet points tied to the report appendix.
sampleRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Extracted from Appendix B page 21 of the public sample PDF as formal appendix structure only; do not infer local emotional eating or prescribe behavior changes.

sampleRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample appendix explains METs and lists physical activities with METs per hour, including rest, walking, gardening, cleaning, cycling, swimming, running, and other activities.
sampleRows.description: Visible appendix pages 22-23 cite Harvard Health Publishing, December 2009, for the physical activity table.
sampleRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Extracted from Appendix C pages 22-23 of the public sample PDF as formal appendix structure only; do not convert to a local exercise prescription.

sampleRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample disclaimer states the report is educational, genetic information should be considered with lifestyle, family history, biomedical data, nutrition, and physical activity, and the information is not intended as a substitute for physician advice.
sampleRows.description: The visible final page also includes privacy/security and Sequencing.com delivery context.
sampleRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Extracted from page 24 of the public sample PDF as limitation structure; local output must preserve non-medical, non-diagnostic, non-treatment boundaries.

dietContext: Use this report as educational diet-genetics context, not as a weight-loss prediction, diet prescription, exercise program, or meal plan.

Treat the GeneInformed DNA Diet sample action points, macronutrient ratios, trait rows, appendices, and disclaimer as observed output structure only unless equivalent validated local evidence is supplied.
Plan diet and weight goals using diet history, activity, labs, medical context, preferences, and professional support.
Do not use genotype alone to predict weight loss, diagnose obesity or metabolic disease, choose supplements, set macronutrient targets, prescribe exercise, or change treatment.

missingInputs: Authenticated marketplace detail-page parity has not been extracted.
No calibrated diet, weight-loss response, obesity-risk, meal-plan, supplement, metabolic, activity-history, lab, or ancestry applicability model is supplied.
No diet history, activity history, BMI, body-composition data, metabolic labs, medication list, medical history, or clinician/dietitian review is supplied.
The public sample PDF does not expose rsID/SNP identifiers for the results-summary gene rows, so no rsIDs are inferred from gene names and genotype-like calls.
uncertainty: Nutrition, weight management, food response, metabolic outcomes, satiety, taste, and eating behavior depend on genetics plus diet history, activity, labs, medical history, medications, environment, and professional review.
Public sample rows are sample-output examples, not local-user probabilities, diet targets, action points, supplement instructions, exercise programming, or treatment guidance.
A single marker should not be used to diagnose food reactions, nutrient deficiency, obesity, metabolic disease, or to prescribe diet or supplement changes.
limitations: This validation result uses derived evidence only; raw genome data is not included.

The report is educational and does not predict weight loss, diagnose obesity or metabolic disease, prescribe a diet, create meal plans, recommend supplements, prescribe exercise, interpret labs, or provide treatment guidance.
Sequencing.com marketplace detail-page parity is still pending even though the public DNA Diet sample PDF rows are extracted.
